In Person Cold Call Script for Driver Operations – Cert III
Compliance Requirements
1) 	You may not use the word, FREE, to describe this program
2) 	You must always use a hedge-word (i.e. almost, basically, virtually) if you
refer to “100% state funding.” (It’s actually 99.33% funding)
3) 	You must disclose the $20 student fee ($10 for concessionals), which can be
paid up front or upon completion of the program.
Compliance-Approved Language-Pattern
Hey, my name _is ___________, from _________ (your companies name)? How you
going today, mate?
Okay, great! Now, mate, we work in conjunction with the state government, and right
now I’ve got a driver operations certificate—Cert 3—available to you that’s being funded
by the state of Queensland. Have you heard about the state funding at all?
Oh—okay! Well, mate, you’re eligible right now for a Certificate 3 for commercial
driving … and basically the entire cost is being funded by the state. Now, this is an
industry standard in Victoria—you can’t get a job anywhere respectable without it—and
now they’re really pushing it in Queensland, so you wanna grab that funding now, while
it’s still there.
Now, normally, it’s a $3,000 course, but with the state funding that’s available, the timing
is perfect. Are you interested?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 4 --

Okay, great, mate! Let me just ask you a couple of quick questions, to make sure you
qualify for the state funding. Now, first, you are a resident of Queensland, correct?
Okay, excellent. (Check it off the application sheet). And, mate, your full name is…?
Okay—excellent. (Your full name) Nice to meet you! (Smile and shake his hand.) And,
mate, do you have any other qualifications that you got after high school … either a Cert
3 or Cert 4?
Okay, excellent. And, lastly, how many years have you been driving for, mate … just a
ballpark?
Okay, excellent! Well, based on what you just said to me, mate, you definitely qualify for
the state funding, mate, so I can get you your Cert 3 for driving operations without you
having to lay out a cent.
Now, mate, getting started is very simple. All I need is some basic information, to fill out
the application, and then we’ll handle everything else after that. But, first, real quick let
me tell you just a little bit more about the actual training, because it’s totally awesome.
It’ll take me less than sixty seconds. Sound good?
Okay, excellent. Now, for starters, the training is done at your convenience, not ours—
meaning, our trainers will come to you, not the other way around, which is how the rest
of the industry does it, and which is a total pain in the ass!
I mean, the key here is to get your qualification with ideally no downtime, so our trainers
will come to you day or night, on weekdays or weekend, and at the place of your
choosing—meaning, they can come to your work, or to your home, or meet you on the
road somewhere; whatever suits you best. Make sense so far?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 4 --

Okay, great. Now, in terms of the actual training, they’re obviously not there to teach you
how to drive. They know you already know how to do that, so they’re not gonna waste
your time. I mean, after ___ years on the job, you probably know 80-90% of what’s on
the qualification, so what the trainers will do is sit down with you and go through
everything, and sign you off on all the things you already know. They call this
“Recognition of Prior Learning”, and it totally streamlines the process, so you don’t
waste any time. Makes sense?
Okay, excellent! And then, for the things that you do need to learn—meaning, the other
10-20%—the trainer will use the process of “gap training,” to fill in any holes. And, bam,
just like that, you’ll have your nationally recognized qualification for driver operations,
which, by the way, you totally deserve to have. I mean, you’re paying for this out of your
tax dollars anyway, so it’s important to us that you get every bit of value out it.
And believe me, mate: if you’re even half as thrilled as the rest of the drivers who’ve
been through this program, then you’re going to be very, very impressed. Sound fair
enough?
Great! So I just need some basic information, to do the application, and then we can
handle everything else for you after that. Now, first, do you have your driver’s license
handy?
As you complete the application, you want to be doing four things:
1) 	Building deeper and deeper rapport
2) 	Reinforcing the value and benefits of the program
3) 	Reinforcing the urgency of doing it now.
4) 	Finish by explaining the student fee and giving them the option to
pay now or after the training.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 4 --

How to Ask for the Student Fee
Okay, great! Now, mate, like I said before, the state pays for basically the entire training,
including the cost of issuing the diploma (should we continue with certification so it
doesn’t cause any confusion?). The tuition itself is just under $3000, which the state pays
us for directly, so there’s absolutely no out of pocket cost to you there whatsoever. The
only thing the state does require is that you pay a nominal student fee, which is only $20,
believe it or not, so it’s pretty irrelevant. But, still, the state wants you to have at least
have a tiny bit of skin in the game, so they make us charge you $20. (Shake your head, as
if it defies logic.)
Anyway, you don’t have to pay it now, if you don’t want to; you can pay it at the end of
the training, when you get your diploma (certificate?); whichever is easiest for you. I
mean, the truth is that we don’t even want to charge you the $20, but the state requires it.
And since its such a small amount, it’s not (missing the end of the sentence??)
Anyway, it’s totally up to you. If you want to pay it now, I can give you a receipt for it, or
we can take care of it at the end of the training, when you get your diploma (certificate?).
Either way, it’s totally cool. You end be getting your Cert 3 for basically the cost of a six-
pack. So you want take care of it now, or when you get your diploma (certificate?)?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 4 --
